observations.text: Exceptionally thorough business_impact analysis — each sub-field contains nuanced, balanced assessment (e.g., financial covers both positive SLA metric improvement and negative churn risk)
8 stakeholder groups identified with specific, differentiated impact per group — far exceeds the minimum of 3
4 well-structured escalations with options and recommendations, covering service levels, contractual SLAs, API versioning, and timezone policy

Business rules distinguish invariants from policy rules with clear justification for each classification
Identifies the subtle insight that on-time rate improvement from wider windows could mask genuine operational problems (measurement risk)

observations.text: Exceptional depth across all 4 business_impact dimensions — financial analysis includes both positive (SLA penalty reduction) and negative (premium tier revenue risk) sides
7 stakeholder groups far exceeds the minimum 3, with granular per-group impact descriptions including Data/Analytics team which is a non-obvious but valuable inclusion
3 well-structured escalations with options and recommendations — covers scope ambiguity, motivation clarity, and B2B contractual risk
Confidence level of 55 is well-calibrated given the missing contract and scope information, with clear separation of high vs low confidence areas
Identifies the OTDR vanity metric trap — wider window mechanically improves numbers without operational improvement, which is a sophisticated insight
Competitive positioning analysis is substantive, referencing industry trend toward precision (Amazon, UPS, FedEx 2-4 hour windows)
Edge cases are thorough — timezone boundaries, same-day delivery, premium tier value erosion, in-flight order migration all identified
Operational impact correctly balances the benefit (routing flexibility) against the cost (masking performance issues)

Rejection reasons add accountability by linking gaps back to specific business rules
Financial analysis captures the nuance of OTD metric inflation masking operational problems — a non-obvious second-order effect
Risk field identifies 5 distinct risks including the less obvious metric gaming and API breaking change risks
